sentence: scrape the front page of a news website and email me a summary
error: Built a coverage gate (stopword-stripped TF-IDF cosine over skill docs, ix-supervised). It catches WHOLLY-disjoint vocab ('teleport to the moon and bake a cake' -> 0.00) but NOT partial content-word collisions: this scrape/email request scores 0.324 vs trace.ingest (>0.08 gate) on 'summary'/'page'/'ingest', so it still compiles. Raising the threshold would false-block thin in-domain requests (synthesis warned: lexical is weakest in the semantic-overlap zone). NEXT INCREMENT: semantic-embedding coverage (ga real embeddings / ix-gpu cosine over embeddings) or a constrained LLM relevance classifier used FAIL-CLOSED (routing role, not self-judge). Second-order dogfood finding, 2026-06-06 — the loop found the limit of its own fix.

Template-time governance gate bypass (PR #77 adversarial review, P1 conf 0.80). governance_gate() substring-scans stage.args AS WRITTEN, but {"from":"upstream[.key]"} refs resolve to the upstream stage's OUTPUT at execution time (ix-pipeline::lower::resolve_from_refs). A stage like cache {operation:{from:'prep.op'}} serializes with no Article-3 trigger word, passes the gate, then the executor resolves operation->'delete' and runs it. Bounded exploitability (an upstream skill must EMIT the trigger string at runtime; a literal would trip that stage's own gate), but the gate is structurally template-only with NO post-resolution check. MITIGATED this PR: removed the 'unbypassable' overclaim; the gate now surfaces from-refs as unvetted_runtime_inputs in its verdict. DURABLE FIX = the `unify` increment: push the constitutional check into ix-pipeline::execute()/the lower compute closure so check_action sees the literal RESOLVED operation just before (desc.fn_ptr). Third-order dogfood finding, 2026-06-06 — the adversarial review found a structural gap in the gate the loop itself built.
